[[Category:Aelaki grammar]]

== Historical baseline ==
Classical [[Aelaki]] texts are parsed as '''Subject-Verb-Object''' (SVO). However, modern speech is almost entirely '''pro-drop''', so the finite verb tends to surface first. The default spoken order is therefore '''V(S)(O)''', with arguments recovered from [[Ki_syllables|Ki clitics]] or context.

== Argument marking with Ki placement ==
{| class="wikitable"
! Verb type !! Prefix Ki (left of stem) !! Suffix Ki (right of stem)
|-
| '''Transitive''' || subject || object
|-
| '''Intransitive (active)''' || subject || –
|-
| '''Intransitive (stative)''' || – || subject
|}

== Imperative template ==
[[Imperative]]s historically employed '''S-O-V''' order. In modern usage they retain a frozen morphology:

: [SUBJ-Ki][OBJ-Ki]-VERB

This pattern is now the default 1st-person futurative:

: '''Thu-bal-zar''' "Let '''me''' catch '''two''' (fish)!"

== Pragmatic notes ==
* First-person speakers strongly prefer the [[Visual|visual past]] for narration of completed actions
* Except in humorous or self-deprecating references, one avoids assigning oneself the [[Mythic|mythical past]]

== Case hierarchy ==
When a verb agrees with multiple arguments, a hierarchy determines which gets the Ki slot:

'''[[Agent]]''' > '''[[Dative]]''' > '''[[Patient]]''' > '''[[Instrumental]]''' > '''[[Possessive]]''' > '''[[Speaker]]'''

The [[Speaker]] case should be indicated with the speaker auxiliary verb if it is overwritten by a higher-ranked case.

== See also ==
* [[Ki_syllables]]
* [[Imperative]]
* [[Agent]]
* [[Patient]]
